data.title: Casella Waste Systems rated Buy in new coverage at TD Cowen
data.description:
Casella Waste Systems (NASDAQ:CWST) on Wednesday was rated Buy in new research coverage by TD Cowen, which pointed to the company’s durable free cash flow, strong pricing power and upside to landfill volume growth as key drivers of future performance.

James Schumm, a research analyst at TD Cowen, noted that Casella’s (NASDAQ:CWST) resilience across economic cycles makes it a standout in the solid waste sector.

“M&A growth and free-cash-flow durability in good or bad economic times makes Casella a particularly attractive investment,” Schumm said.

Tariff impact limited, inflation could play to Casella’s strengths

Despite rising concerns around potential tariff-related cost pressures, Schumm sees minimal risk to Casella’s (NASDAQ:CWST) core operations.

“We think tariffs will have limited impact on the solid waste industry,” he said. “But if tariffs spur a wave of inflation, we think Casella is likely the most insulated, as we estimate roughly 75% of their business is not price-restricted and can increase prices immediately.”

Landfill volumes set for a comeback

After years of stagnation, landfill volumes may finally be on the upswing.

“Landfill revenues have been virtually flat since 2019 despite annual price growth of 6%–7%,” Schumm said.

But a competing Long Island landfill’s recent closure may reverse that trend, potentially pushing Casella’s (CWST) landfill volumes up 18% over time, with an approximately 8% boost expected in 2025.

Conservative 2025 guidance leaves room for upside

Casella’s (CWST) recently issued 2025 guidance was labeled “peer leading” by Schumm, but he believes it understates the company’s true potential.

“We think 2025 revenues have the potential to be 500 basis points higher than the initial guidance,” he said, suggesting this could translate into an approximately 600-basis-point beat on earnings before interest taxes, interest, depreciation and amortization when accounting for operating leverage and a more favorable mix.

Focus on free cash flow over margins

In evaluating performance drivers for solid waste firms, TD Cowen prioritizes free cash flow per share and margin expansion for earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization. Casella (CWST), Schumm said, is likely to outperform peers in free-cash-flow growth, although its ability to close the margin gap remains a key question given the margin dilution from its aggressive M&A strategy.

Margin expansion catalysts on the horizon

TD Cowen sees a clear path to margin improvement in 2025, estimating a $20 million ebitda uplift even before any price-driven growth. This includes:

  • $10–11 million from rising landfill volumes,
  • $5 million from truck fleet automation efforts,
  • $4 million from upgrades at the Willimantic recycling facility.

Valuation and outlook

TD Cowen set a price target of $130 a share on Casella (CWST), implying about 25% upside from current levels.

Casella (CWST) currently trades at 17 times and 15 times 2025 and 2026 enterprise-value-to-ebitda multiples, respectively — levels consistent with its five-year average. TD Cowen’s discounted-cash-flow-based price target of $130, using a 7.5% weighted average cost of capital, reflects confidence in the company’s long-term fundamentals and upside potential.

“With strong FCF growth, limited exposure to tariffs and several margin tailwinds on the horizon, Casella is well-positioned to outperform,” Schumm said.

data.title: Quest launches test to confirm amyloid brain pathology in Alzheimer's patients
data.description:
• Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) said on Wednesday it has launched a laboratory blood test to confirm amyloid brain pathology due to Alzheimer's disease in patients with mild cognitive impairment or dementia.
  • Researchers at Quest shared new data showing that when the test was adjusted to catch 91% of true cases and correctly rule out 91% of non-cases, it was 89% accurate in predicting both who likely has the condition and who likely doesn’t.
  • The percentage of patients with likelihood of indeterminate risk was 15%, which decreased to 10% when ApoE test results were included, the company said.

data.title: Trump tariffs turn tech 'global supply chain upside down': Wedbush
data.description:
The swath of tariffs levied by U.S. President Donald Trump against 86 trade partners, led by a 104% tariff on China, has upended the global tech supply chain. The uncertainty will likely prompt most tech companies to withhold guidance during the upcoming earnings season, according to Wedbush.  

"At the current situation we do not expect any tech companies to give any guidance on the 1Q conference calls over the next month, including Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), given too much uncertainty," said Wedbush analysts, led by Daniel Ives, in a Wednesday investor note.

The new tariffs took effect today. China has responded with its own 84% tariff on U.S. goods, effective on Thursday, April 10.

"The US' practice of escalating tariffs on China is a mistake on top of a mistake, which seriously infringes on China's legitimate rights and interests, seriously damages the rules-based multilateral trading system, and seriously impacts the stability of the global economic order," said China's Office of the Tariff Commission of the State Council.

"In our view, these tariffs have already created significant enterprise demand destruction out of the gates as Cap-Ex and new projects are being halted across the US until this chaotic situation is better understood by CEOs and business leaders around the world," Ives added. "Any big ticket items are being heavily scrutinized and analyzed to understand did enterprise hardware ordered by an IT product manager last month now just double in price being shipped from China as an example."

At this point, investors will need to maintain patience, "toss out" the new few quarters, and await some type of normalcy to return to the market in 2026, Ives said.  

"Apple and Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) remain two core tech names to own and despite this massive near-term uncertainty it does not change their installed base, technology leadership, and long-term growth opportunities, especially with Nvidia leading the AI industry," he said.

By the close of markets on Tuesday, multiple semiconductor companies' shares had already reached multi-year lows.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Microbot Medical rises as robotic surgical system succeeds in pivotal trial
data.description:
Shares of Microbot Medical (NASDAQ:MBOT) rose in the premarket on Wednesday after the MedTech firm announced that a pivotal trial for its Liberty endovascular surgical robotic system reached the primary endpoint.

Presenting data from its ACCESS-PVI at a medical conference on interventional radiology, Microbot (NASDAQ:MBOT) said Liberty indicated a 100% success rate in terms of robotic navigation in all 20 cases treated, meeting the study’s main goal.

According to Braintree, Massachusetts-based MBOT, ACCESS-PVI’s operator arm demonstrated a mean 92% relative reduction in radiation exposure, and there were no adverse device events throughout the study’s follow-up period.

The company added that Liberty, an investigational device awaiting FDA’s 510((k)) clearance, indicated a median of three minutes of robotic navigation time to reach target.

“As we shift focus to building our commercial capabilities and preparing for launch, we are confident that LIBERTY® will be well received in the market,” CEO Harel Gadot added.

data.title: Orbit's Electronics Group reports Q1 bookings in excess of $4.7M
data.description:
• Orbit International (OTCPK:ORBT) has announced that its Electronics Group received orders during the Q1 of 2025 totaling in excess of $4.7M.
  • Deliveries for these awards have already commenced and will continue through the second quarter of 2026.
  • "We continue to be confident that in 2025, we will continue to both receive follow-on business for our VPX power supplies and deliver new designs that will expand our reach in the marketplace for this technology." said Mitchell Binder, President and CEO.

data.title: Ronn signs agreement for regional joint venture in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
• Ronn (OTC PINK:RONN) has entered into an agreement with an international intermediary group specializing in Saudia Arabia to form a 50/50 regional joint venture, which would establish a significant operating presence for the company within the Kingdom.
  • "It will establish our manufacturing and production in Saudi Arabia as well as the US where the collaboration with Saudi Arabia is a powerful collaboration at this time," said Ronn Ford, CEO of Ronn.
  • The proposed joint venture in Saudi Arabia is expected to focus on localized manufacturing and regional deployment of Ronn Inc.'s hydrogen technologies, including hydrogen-powered supercars, commercial mobility platforms, hydrogen trucks, and modular fueling infrastructure.
  • Plans for the phase 1 of the agreement currently include the establishment of a regional headquarters in the Kingdom, in collaboration with a group of strategic partners with proven success in rail technology, real estate, transportation, and public-private mega infrastructure. Further details on phase 2 and phase 3 will be disclosed as the joint venture structure and operating scope are finalized, the company said.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock index futures fall sharply as China puts 84% tariffs on U.S. products
data.description:
Stock index futures fell sharply on Wednesday as China reportedly slapped 84% tariffs on U.S. products after the Trump administration went ahead with its 104% levy on Chinese goods.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -1.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -1.8%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 12 basis points to 4.38%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.77%.

In response to the U.S.'s 104% tariffs on Chinese goods, Beijing will raise its tariff on U.S. goods to 84%, effective April 10, the government said on Wednesday, according to media reports.

Another topsy-turvy trading session on Wall Street saw the major averages rally furiously at the open, then steadily give up gains and turn negative, followed by a period of fluctuation. They then decidedly turned lower and eventually ended deep in the red.

"Several triggers drove a sharp turn lower in market sentiment as the day went on. First, we had confirmation from U.S. officials that the additional 50% tariff that Trump threatened China with on Monday would be imposed, dashing any remaining hopes for a last-minute extension before the deadline," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"The market turmoil has shown no sign of letting up over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 edging increasingly close to bear market territory, having now shed -18.9% since its peak in mid-February. Moreover, futures are still pointing towards further losses today,"  Allen said.

Trump on Tuesday also said that the U.S. will soon introduce a "major" tariff on pharmaceutical imports.

"Trump implemented an extraordinary tax increase on US consumers overnight. If the 104% tax increase on goods from China is not reversed, it is likely to push the US into recession more rapidly," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

For years, the U.S. persuaded foreigners to exchange U.S. government promises for valuable goods and services. That exchange is threatened—despite equities falling yesterday, bond yields rose sharply, Donovan added.

The results of $39B, 10-year note auction and the FOMC minutes will land later in the day on Wednesday.

data.title: Altus Power shareholders approve $5/share acquisition by TPG
data.description:
Altus Power (NYSE:AMPS) said Wednesday that shareholders voted to approve the previously announced agreement for the company's acquisition by TPG through its TPG Rise Climate Transition Infrastructure strategy.

Altus Power (NYSE:AMPS) shareholders will receive $5.00 in cash for each share of Altus Power Class A common stock owned immediately prior to the effective time of the merger; the deal valued the company at ~$2.2B including outstanding debt.

By combining TPG Rise Climate’s investment capabilities with Altus Power’s (AMPS) domain expertise in commercial-scale solar, Altus had said it will "be able to scale its operations more rapidly to meet the surging demand for increased power generation."

data.title: BitFuFu reports sequentially higher bitcoin holdings at March-end
data.description:
• Crytocurrency miner BitFuFu (NASDAQ:FUFU) announced on Wednesday bitcoin holdings of 1,847 as of March 31, an increase of 47 BTC from the end of February.
  • Shares were -4.82% pre-market at $3.95.
  • The company produced 234 bitcoins for the month, 58 from self-mining operations and 176 for cloud-mining customers.
  • Total hashrate under management stood at 20.6 EH/s as of March-end.

data.title: Aditxt regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• Aditxt (NASDAQ:ADTX) said it has received a notice from Nasdaq stating that the firm has regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement.
  • Press Release
